summary: Matched the Mr. Game & Watch Panic item translation unit by replacing asm/melee/it/items/itgamewatchpanic.s with C, marking it Matching in configure.py, and switching obj_files.mk to build the C object. The PR also cleaned up declarations by moving GameWatch Panic prototypes into itgamewatchpanic.h, removing them from the broad it_27CF.h header, adding the needed it_8027CE64 prototype to it_2725.h, and updating ftGw_SpecialLw.c to include the item-specific header. No PR body or review comments were present in the slice.
what_changed: Deleted the 243-line assembly file asm/melee/it/items/itgamewatchpanic.s and replaced the previously empty src/melee/it/items/itgamewatchpanic.c with a 107-line C implementation.

Implemented the Game & Watch Panic item spawn function it_802C7D60 using SpawnItem, It_Kind_GameWatch_Panic, copied previous/current position, zeroed velocity and damage, set parent gobj fields, enabled x44_flag.bits.b0, called Item_80268B18, then initialized the item with Item_8026AB54, db_80225DD8, and it_8027CE64.
Implemented item callbacks it_802C7E64, it_802C7E94, it_802C7EE0, it_802C7F00, it_802C7F20, static bool it_802C7FAC, and it_802C8018 in C.
Defined ItemStateTable it_803F79A0 in C with two states, both using it_802C7FAC as the callback and NULL for the remaining callback slots.
Marked it_802C7FAC as static in C and changed its symbol entry in config/GALE01/symbols.txt from global to local.
Changed configure.py from Object(NonMatching, "melee/it/items/itgamewatchpanic.c") to Object(Matching, "melee/it/items/itgamewatchpanic.c").
Changed obj_files.mk to build $(BUILD_DIR)/src/melee/it/items/itgamewatchpanic.c.o instead of the old asm object.
Moved GameWatch Panic-specific prototypes out of src/melee/it/items/it_27CF.h and into src/melee/it/items/itgamewatchpanic.h.
Updated ftGw_SpecialLw.c to include "it/items/itgamewatchpanic.h" instead of the broader "it/items/it_27CF.h".
Added the prototype for it_8027CE64(Item_GObj*, Item_GObj*, s32) to src/melee/it/it_2725.h.
smart_moves: Kept the item-specific API in itgamewatchpanic.h rather than leaving prototypes in the broad it_27CF.h header, improving include locality for future item work.
Changed the symbol scope for it_802C7FAC to local after making it a static helper, aligning config/GALE01/symbols.txt with C linkage.

Reused existing item and fighter helper APIs instead of open-coding behavior: GET_ITEM, Item_80268B18, Item_8026AB54, db_80225DD8, it_8027CE64, ftGw_SpecialLw_ItemPanicSetFlag, ftGw_SpecialLw_ItemCheckPanicRemove, ftLib_800865CC, Item_80268E5C, Item_802694CC, and it_8026B894.

Preserved the assembly-visible behavior of the spawn routine by explicitly mirroring stack-built SpawnItem initialization, including duplicated parent fields, copied Vec3 position, zeroed velocity, damage zero, and bitfield setup.
Used a conditional expression in it_802C7FAC to match the control flow where a missing owner behaves as removable/true, while a present owner delegates to ftGw_SpecialLw_ItemCheckPanicRemove.
Used PAD_STACK(8) in it_802C7FAC, indicating a targeted stack-layout/matching tactic rather than a purely semantic rewrite.
decomp_lessons: When converting an item asm file to C, update all three build surfaces together: delete the asm source, switch obj_files.mk from asm object to C object, and mark the C file Matching in configure.py.
If a helper callback is only referenced by an item state table inside the same translation unit, make it static and adjust symbols.txt scope to local.

For item spawner matches, reproduce SpawnItem initialization order and fields carefully: kind, previous/current position, facing direction, damage, velocity, parent pointers, flags, and x40 can all affect matching.
Item owner interactions should use the established owner field through GET_ITEM and guard NULL owners before calling fighter-specific callbacks.
Header cleanup can be part of matching work: move narrow item declarations to the item-specific header and include that specific header at call sites.

Existing item-common wrappers such as it_8026B724, it_8026B73C, and it_8026B894 often decompile to thin forwarding callbacks and should be preserved as simple wrappers when the asm shows direct branches/calls.
Ground/air animation selection can be expressed with ftLib_800865CC into ip->ground_or_air followed by Item_80268E5C with the correct animation index and ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE.
naming_conventions: Item-specific source and header names follow the pattern src/melee/it/items/itgamewatchpanic.c and itgamewatchpanic.h.
Address-derived function names were retained: it_802C7D60, it_802C7E64, it_802C7E94, it_802C7EE0, it_802C7F00, it_802C7F20, it_802C7FAC, and it_802C8018.
The item state table retained the address-derived global name it_803F79A0.
The internal state callback it_802C7FAC was declared as static bool in the C file and omitted from the public item header.
Function prototypes in headers include address comments such as /* 2C7D60 */ and /* 3F79A0 */.
The item kind enum spelling used in the spawn routine is It_Kind_GameWatch_Panic.
Fighter-specific callback names use the ftGw_SpecialLw_ prefix, as in ftGw_SpecialLw_ItemPanicSetFlag and ftGw_SpecialLw_ItemCheckPanicRemove.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced the assembly object with a C object only after the C implementation matched, reflected by configure.py changing NonMatching to Matching.
Used PAD_STACK(8) in it_802C7FAC to satisfy stack layout/codegen requirements.
Made it_802C7FAC static and changed its symbols.txt scope from global to local to match linkage and symbol expectations.
Modeled the assembly's copied Vec3 values with spawn_item.prev_pos = *pos followed by spawn_item.pos = spawn_item.prev_pos.
Modeled chained zero stores for velocity as spawn_item.vel.x = spawn_item.vel.y = spawn_item.vel.z = 0.0F.
Modeled the assembly bit insertion into the SpawnItem flags as spawn_item.x44_flag.bits.b0 = true.
Preserved NULL-owner branches in callbacks, especially owner checks before ftGw_SpecialLw_ItemPanicSetFlag and ftGw_SpecialLw_ItemCheckPanicRemove.
Used local variables such as HSD_GObj* gobj, Item* ip, and int* attr to express the same sequence of loads and calls from the original asm.
follow_up_queries: Find other item PRs that made an item state callback static and changed symbols.txt scope to local.
Find other Game & Watch item matches that moved prototypes from it_27CF.h to item-specific headers.
Find PRs using PAD_STACK in item callbacks for matching.
Find other SpawnItem initialization patterns for article/item spawner functions.
Find remaining NonMatching Game & Watch item files near itgamewatchbreath, itgamewatchjudge, itgamewatchrescue, and itgamewatchchef.
